read_policy: Use this file for active backlog, next actions, scanner counts, and stable debt ids. Do not reread archived markdown history unless a debt id points there.

notes: The widget timing matches are the intentional centralized pumpFeatureUi helper plus two calendar test pumps queued for a later testability pass.
Feature tappable scanner now skips built-in labeled buttons, tooltipped controls, and tappables already wrapped in Semantics or Tooltip.
Legacy spacing scanner is split between canonical 4-point migration candidates and intentional fine-grained compatibility helpers.
Canonical spacing migration should be piecemeal and touch-driven.
Raw error-surface candidates are clean after the hard CatchErrorText removal and direct primitive migration.
Stream lifecycle watch audit on 2026-05-06 found only one-shot auth/payment protocol timeout usage, not Firestore snapshot listener timeouts.
Error UI watch audit on 2026-05-06 found no CatchErrorText regression and zero scanner raw error-surface candidates.

pending.title: Fix profile tab and preview tab blank rendering
pending.summary: The null-profile blank branch now renders an explicit missing-profile state, profile canonical spacing is migrated, and ProfileScreen currently uses NestedScrollView with native TabBarView paging for the Edit/Preview tabs. The previous single-CustomScrollView registry text was stale. On 2026-05-06 the iPhone debug loop reproduced a profile edit-sheet lifecycle crash (`TextEditingController was used after being disposed`) during profile interaction; `profile_edit_sheet.dart` now owns text/int controllers inside the modal route, regression tests cover Done dismissal, and nullable single-choice sheets have root-cause coverage. The Profile NestedScrollView overlap contract is explicit in code, but physical testing still shows an unresolved bug: when the user scrolls upward from the Preview tab at the top of the internal profile card, the outer Profile header does not reliably return unless the gesture starts near the tab row. Do not mark this complete until the preview-card leading overscroll expands the outer header continuously on device.

pending.title: Align onboarding and edit-profile field coverage
pending.summary: Shared profile validation now covers name, required display name, date of birth, phone, optional email, optional Instagram, height bounds, and open-ended age display/storage. Onboarding initializes `displayName` from first name and explains that first name starts the public display name while last name stays private. Edit profile exposes `Display name` as the first editable About field and keeps identity fields (date of birth and gender) readonly. Public profile rendering prefers `displayName`, then first name, then legacy full-name first token, so last name is not shown publicly. Profile edit sheets now save before dismissing and show mutation loading/error feedback. All nullable single-choice edit fields have no-selected-chip coverage when empty; domain decode preserves omitted optional enum fields as null; `ChipField` has a direct empty single-select rendering test; immediate-save single-choice sheets show a visible saving state; and failed single-choice saves clear optimistic pending selection before showing inline error. Remaining field coverage is intentionally asymmetric: onboarding captures only low-friction signup fields (name, date of birth, phone, gender, interested-in genders, Instagram, photos, pace range, preferred distances, and running reasons), while edit profile additionally exposes email, height, bio, city, age range, occupation, company, education, religion, languages, relationship goal, drinking, smoking, workout, diet, and children. Do not expand onboarding with those optional fields unless product explicitly accepts the added signup friction.

pending.title: Polish shared public-facing profile card
pending.summary: Implementation polish pass completed on 2026-05-06. Swipes, Profile Preview, and Public Profile still share one `ProfileCard` rendering path. The card remains dark and immersive in both light and dark app themes through `ProfileCardPalette`, shows display name + age + optional city on the hero, moves relationship goal into detail chips, promotes the bio prompt ahead of running stats, and uses a branded missing-photo fallback. `_RunningIdentityCard` is now the single canonical `RUN PROFILE` section; the redundant lower `ProfileRunningSection` was removed. Non-hero photos are inset and rounded inside the card while the hero remains full-bleed. Remaining work is physical-device visual review before deciding whether to continue more opinionated refinements.

pending.title: Add optional sound to full-screen celebration moments
pending.summary: Full-screen celebration moments now share `CatchCelebrationScreen` and `CelebrationEffectsController`, with haptics enabled by default for run creation, run signup/payment confirmation, participant self-check-in, and matches. Sound is intentionally deferred. When product is ready, add sound through `CelebrationEffectsController` only, choose app-owned audio assets/package, respect mute/silent/accessibility expectations, and keep widget tests deterministic through an injected fake effects controller.

pending.title: Apply callable rate limits consistently
pending.summary: Closed on 2026-05-06. `verifyRazorpayPayment`, `cancelRunSignUp`, `joinRunWaitlist`, `blockUser`, `unblockUser`, and `requestAccountDeletion` now enforce shared `checkRateLimit` before expensive or destructive work. `updateUserProfile` is now configured and enforced at 60/minute so field-by-field profile edits remain ergonomic while write loops are bounded. Watch future callables for the same App Check + auth + Zod validation + rate-limit ordering.

pending.title: Design backend-owned notification fan-out
pending.summary: Before adding more notifications, decide whether durable in-app activity/timeline events live in a server-owned Firestore collection or whether specific moments remain push-only. Candidate moments: new message, match, upcoming run reminder, signup/waitlist promotion, run cancellation/schedule change, and club/hosted-run updates. Client writes to any notification timeline should be denied; fan-out should happen in Functions.

pending.title: Move relationship reads off compatibility arrays
pending.summary: Relationship/action documents now exist for run-club memberships, run participations, saved runs, and match-scoped messages. Run detail saved-state reads now use `savedRuns/{uid_runId}`. Dashboard recommendations, Run Clubs list/detail membership state, and Run Map recommendations now read `runClubMemberships` rather than `users.joinedRunClubIds` or `runClubs.memberUserIds`; `RecommendedRunsQuery` gives recommendation providers stable value-equality keys. Run detail now reads the current viewer's booking/waitlist/attendance/review-gate state from `runParticipations/{runId_uid}` instead of compatibility participant arrays. Shared `watchSignedUpRunsProvider` and `watchAttendedRunsProvider` now query `runParticipations` by user/status and then watch matching run documents, so Dashboard, Calendar, Run Map, Activity, and Swipe Hub consume edge-backed run streams through the existing provider seam. Host attendance management derives roster/check-in state from `runParticipations` through `AttendanceSheetViewModel`. Swipe candidate generation, swipe empty-state attendance gating, and run recap attendee/checked-in state now use `runParticipations`. Run participation count projections are explicit `Run` fields (`bookedCount`, `waitlistedCount`, `checkedInCount`) maintained by Functions, while exact roster surfaces (`WhoIsRunning`, `HostRunManageScreen`) use `RunParticipationRoster` from edge docs. Production UI no longer directly reads `runs.signedUpUserIds`/`waitlistUserIds`/`attendedUserIds`; compatibility arrays remain only in generated serialization, backend write compatibility, migration tooling, and tests until beta migration/reset policy is finalized.

pending.title: Add seeded tests and count validation for relationship migration apply
pending.summary: `tool/firestore_relationship_migration.mjs` supports dry-run and apply modes for creating relationship docs and copying legacy chat messages. Before running apply on shared beta data, add seeded fixture tests for duplicates, missing docs, deleted users, legacy chats, and count mismatches, and make apply output validate aggregate counts instead of only reporting planned writes.

pending.title: Rewrite deletion and anonymization around relationship-doc queries
pending.summary: Relationship docs make deletes query-driven. Rewrite account deletion to query runClubMemberships by uid, runParticipations by uid, savedRuns by uid, reviews/payments by user, matches by participant, swipes by source/target, public profile, private profile, Storage, and Auth. Add run deletion and club deletion plans that use runParticipations/runClubMemberships instead of scanning parent arrays.

pending.title: Audit retained tab and route streams for lifecycle ownership
pending.summary: Known Firestore listener lifecycle issues are resolved and this queue is now watch-only. Dashboard booked-runs and run-club streams no longer use idle Stream.timeout, and no remaining lib `.timeout` usage targets Firestore snapshot listeners. Home pauses booked-runs while inactive, AppShell no longer prewarms the Clubs list stream, Clubs/Catches/Chats/Profile tab roots gate screen-owned providers while inactive, run-club stream families are autoDispose, and route-owned providers for run detail, chat messages, match detail, payment history, and blocked users have auto-dispose tests. Keep shell-level streams only when they drive shell-wide UI such as auth, current user, connectivity, FCM, or unread badges. Future realtime streams must be auto-dispose, retained-tab gated, or explicitly documented as global/prewarmed with read-cost rationale.

pending.title: Create and migrate to branded app error surfaces
pending.summary: The canonical branded app-facing error primitive family now exists: `CatchErrorState`, `CatchErrorScaffold`, `CatchSliverErrorState`, `CatchInlineErrorState`, `showCatchErrorSnackBar`, and `appErrorMessage` / `appErrorTitle`. `CatchErrorText` has been deleted rather than retained as a compatibility layer. `docs/error-handling-audit.md` owns the app error catalogue, the rule for when to add sealed `AppException` subclasses, naming conventions, and an Error 101 guide. First-batch and hard-migration coverage includes Dashboard, Profile, Run Clubs list/detail, Chats list/thread messages, Catches hub, swipe queue load failures, payments, routing wrappers, run detail, run map, attendance, filters, and run recap. `tool/widget_cleanup_scan.sh` now reports zero raw app-facing error surface candidates. Keep this queue on watch for regressions, and handle likely future catalogue candidates as touched: image upload/storage, reusable validation, external action, and location failures.

pending.title: Keep docs index and registry synchronized
pending.summary: Whenever a durable doc owner is added, renamed, archived, deleted, or assigned a new recurring rule, update docs/README.md, doc_versions.json, doc_summaries.json, rules.json, and any affected owner doc in the same pass.

completed.title: Unify high-emotion success moments under full-screen celebrations
completed.summary: Added `CatchCelebrationScreen` and `CelebrationEffectsController`, migrated host run creation, free and paid user signup confirmation, participant self-check-in, and match creation to full-screen celebration surfaces, and kept host attendance out of the celebration flow. Added an injectable `RunCheckInLocationService` seam so self-check-in remains testable without platform location plugins.

completed.title: Split Home into Dashboard and Activity tabs
completed.summary: Home now follows the Profile-style tab architecture. `DashboardScreen` owns the route-local `TabController`, `NestedScrollView`, collapsible Home header, pinned `Dashboard`/`Activity` tab row, and native `TabBarView` paging. Existing dashboard widgets were extracted into dashboard sliver bodies, Activity moved out of Dashboard content, and the Activity tab renders a timeline-style feed with branded inline retry errors.

completed.title: Audit remaining feature tappable candidates
completed.summary: Reviewed the 24 syntactic tappable candidates. Existing controls already had user-visible text, tooltips, or Semantics wrappers, so the useful fix was scanner refinement. The scanner now reports zero unresolved tappable candidates.

completed.title: Piecemeal legacy spacing migration
completed.summary: Migrated all canonical 4-point Sizes.p* presentation/widget candidates to CatchSpacing.s*. The scanner now reports zero canonical candidates; fine-grained p2/p6/p10/p14/p18 compatibility helpers remain watch-only.
